name: Leaf Development Process
description: Leaf Development Process in Plants. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A13[Shoot Apical Meristem] --> B13[Leaf Primordium Initiation]
    B13 --> C13[Adaxial-Abaxial Patterning]
    C13 --> D13[Lamina Outgrowth]
    D13 --> E13[Vein Formation]
    E13 --> F13[Cell Expansion]
    F13 --> G13[Differentiation]
    G13 --> H13[Mature Leaf]
    H13 --> I13[Senescence]
    
    style A13 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B13 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C13 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style D13 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E13 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F13 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G13 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style H13 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
    style I13 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Leaf Development Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Leaf development: a paradigm for plant organogenesis
Adaxial-abaxial polarity specifies leaf dorsal-ventral identity in Arabidopsis
sources.authors: Eshed, Y., Baum, S.F., Bowman, J.L.

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
